ops.body:
## Context

Routing-v4 follow-up, user-approved plan: can gpt-5.6-luna (or terra) replace sonnet-5 as the recon lane ("Recon and routine subagent work" in the Models table)? User decision rule: terra dropped on research evidence; routing change auto-applies ONLY on a clean gate-met win; partial/mixed reports first. Result: **MIXED → reported first; user then approved the partial lane, APPLIED same day (see bottom).**

## Terra (no live runs)

Dropped on public evidence: Artificial Analysis finds luna Pareto-dominates terra at every cost point ("Luna and Sol are always on the Pareto frontier ahead of Terra"); terra owns the worst open codex issues — early-exit on tool-required search (openai/codex#32162, reproduced 3/3) and xhigh cost blowups (#32606 cluster).

## Design

12 briefs × 3 repos (cc-skills, captain-hook, fusekit) × 5 shapes probing publicly-reported failure modes: 3 exhaustive enumerations (MRCR-cliff probe), 3 subsystem traces, 2 config/wiring locates, 2 cross-cutting pattern sweeps, 2 negative-space (honest answer ≈ "nothing"; hallucination/early-exit probe). Keys: 12 opus-xhigh builders → 186 items, 100% mechanical cite-resolution, 0 ambiguity flags. Arms (48 runs, 0 DNFs): Explore(sonnet) incumbent · Explore(haiku) floor · codex gpt-5.6-luna at high and xhigh via the pinned quiet exec (`-C <repo>`, danger-full-access, 600s cap). Judging: 2 blind opus-xhigh judges per brief, labels rotated per brief, every cite re-resolved live; 4 disagreement cells adjudicated — all counting-convention spreads, none flip any sonnet-vs-luna comparison.

## Results (judge-averaged)

| arm | recall | false cites | conclusions | early exits | usefulness | median secs (contended) | $/brief measured |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Explore sonnet | 167/173 (96.5%) | 1 (minor miscite) | 12/12 | 0 | 4.96 | 1175 | $1.84 std / $1.23 intro |
| Explore haiku | 151.5/173 (87.6%) | 9 | 9/12 | 0 | 4.04 | 1342 | $0.36 |
| luna high | 156/173 (90.2%) | 0 | 12/12 | 0 | 4.79 | 60 | $0.11 (+~$0.26 sonnet-low wrapper) |
| luna xhigh | 167/173 (96.5%) | 0 | 11/12 | 0 | 4.67 | 74 | $0.13 (+~$0.26 wrapper) |

Per-shape recall — enumeration: S 24/24, H 24/24, L-hi 24/24, **L-x 23/24**; trace: S 71/74, H 58.5, **L-hi 59**, L-x 70; config: S 16/19, H 15 (+8 false cites), L-hi 17, **L-x 18 (won outright — a judge's "most complete and precise output", 12/12)**; pattern: 56/56 all except H 54; negative: all luna runs clean, zero invented hits.

Latency, solo pair on a quiet machine (ch-trace): **sonnet 230s vs luna-xhigh 48s (4.8×)**. The contended batch gap (15–20×) overstates single-run speedup but is itself real for fan-outs: luna degrades less under parallel load because the work runs server-side while local Explore agents contend for exec (syspolicyd serialization).

## Gate verdict

Cheaper ✓ (~80%) · faster ✓ (4.8× solo) · zero early-exits/DNFs ✓ · precision ✓ (0 false cites in 24 luna runs) · **enum recall ≥ sonnet ✗** — luna-xhigh reported 3-of-4 test functions on ccs-enum and confidently concluded "three" (the predicted false-certainty failure mode; luna-high found all 4 on the same brief). luna-high passes the enumeration letter but fails the spirit: deep-trace collapse (fk-trace 22/35 — missed fusekit's entire mux health path). No single luna config achieves a clean win.

## Findings

1. Luna's recon effort floor is **xhigh** — high is not recon-grade on deep traces.
2. Luna-xhigh's failure mode is the dangerous one: silent omission with a confident wrong count, exactly what disqualifies unsupervised open-ended exhaustive recon.
3. Haiku (the silent Explore default this hook ecosystem guards against) is validated unfit: 87.6% recall, 9 false cites, 3 wrong conclusions — the Explore→sonnet rewrite hook (captain-hook models.py:247) earns its keep.
4. Fast-mode correction: codex Fast mode officially supports only GPT-5.5/5.4; `service_tier=fast` is inert on 5.6 (kept pinned — harmless, invariant-tested; API "priority" is a separate 2×-billed knob). Luna's measured speed is its own.
5. MRCR-cliff caveat: these repos (~270-400 files) are far below the long-context regime where luna's 41.3% MRCR score bites; monorepo-scale recon remains untested and higher-risk.

## Adopted wording (user-approved, APPLIED 2026-07-13)

sonnet-5 row + sol-row parenthetical + codex SKILL.md variants + codex-wrapper.md: bounded recon sweeps — config/wiring locates, crisp-scope enumerations, pattern sweeps — may route to gpt-5.6-luna via codex at **xhigh only** when speed or fan-out cost matters (matched or beat sonnet there at ~5× speed, ~80% lower cost, zero false cites); open-ended exhaustive recon, deep traces, and anything where a silent miss is costly stay sonnet.

**Applied**: cc-skills 9843473c (canonical rows, codex SKILL/wrapper, test pins "bounded recon sweeps" across all three surfaces, base-conventions paraphrase; codex 0.14.0, repo-bootstrap 0.51.3) + 3a0a67e7 (CLAUDE.md render, rows byte-identical) — both CI green; plugin caches updated at user scope; captain-hook untouched (no luna wording in the pack); fleet CLAUDE.md picks it up via the daily guides cron. Routing guidance doc: 70cf669c.

## Provenance

Workflows: wf_6a74950d-03c (keys), wf_393bac54-9b1 (arms+judges: 72 agents, 3.65M subagent tokens, 0 errors), wf_2c928196-670 (solo latency pair). Research: Artificial Analysis / Vellum / Kingy gpt-5.6 benchmarks (luna Coding Agent Index 74.6-75, MRCR 41.3%), openai/codex #32162 #32606 #31967 #31882, OpenAI pricing ($1/$6 luna vs Sonnet 5 $2/$10 intro → $3/$15) and Codex speed doc (fast mode = 5.5/5.4 only). Related: memory model-routing-convention v4.1 (mirrored as note 26c429e); v4 decision note 8b90a011.
